provide Parent written notice of the board’s intention to make a change in recommendation or to terminate the merger agreement with a description of the terms of the superior proposal

event_claims.description: Under the merger agreement, the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives may not solicit, initiate, knowingly encourage or facilitate alternative proposals or related inquiries, discussions or offers.
event_claims.quote_text:
Under the merger agreement, the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives may not:

•

solicit, initiate or knowingly encourage or facilitate the making or submission of an alternative proposal

event_claims.description: Under the merger agreement, the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives may not participate in discussions or negotiations regarding an alternative proposal or furnish nonpublic information in connection with an alternative proposal, except to notify the person of the non-solicitation provisions.
event_claims.quote_text: participate in any discussions or negotiations regarding an alternative proposal with, or furnish any nonpublic information regarding the Company or its subsidiaries in connection with an alternative proposal to, any person that has made, or is considering making an alternative proposal

event_claims.description: Under the merger agreement, the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives may not enter into any letter of intent, agreement in principle, merger or acquisition agreement or other agreement relating to or providing for an alternative proposal, except permitted confidentiality agreements.
event_claims.quote_text: enter into any letter of intent, agreement in principle, merger or acquisition agreement or any other agreement relating to or providing for any alternative proposal

event_claims.description: Before the required stockholder vote, if the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that the board determines constitutes or could reasonably be expected to result in a superior proposal, the Company may provide nonpublic information after entering into an acceptable confidentiality agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: provide nonpublic information to the third party after entering into a confidentiality agreement acceptable under the terms of the merger agreement

event_claims.description: Before the required stockholder vote, if the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that the board determines constitutes or could reasonably be expected to result in a superior proposal, the Company may engage in discussions or negotiations with that third party regarding the alternative proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: engage in discussions or negotiations with a third party with respect to the alternative proposal

event_claims.description: For the board to change its recommendation or for the Company to terminate the merger agreement to accept a superior proposal, the Company must provide Parent written notice describing the superior proposal terms.
event_claims.quote_text: provide Parent written notice of the board’s intention to make a change in recommendation or to terminate the merger agreement with a description of the terms of the superior proposal

event_claims.description: For the board to change its recommendation or for the Company to terminate the merger agreement to accept a superior proposal, the Company must provide Parent written reasons for doing so.
event_claims.quote_text: provide to Parent in writing its reasons for making the change in recommendation or for terminating the merger agreement

event_claims.description: The Company must provide Parent three business days to propose changes to the merger agreement terms, extended for material changes in the superior proposal, and negotiate in good faith during that period.
event_claims.quote_text: provide Parent with three business days to propose changes to the terms of the merger agreement (which will be extended for additional two business day periods for any material change in the superior proposal), and during this period negotiate with Parent in good faith to make adjustments to the terms and conditions of the merger agreement

event_claims.description: After the matching period, the board must determine that the superior proposal remains superior to the merger proposal after considering Parent’s proposed revisions.
event_claims.quote_text: the board must determine that the superior proposal remains superior to the merger proposal

event_claims.description: If the Company terminates the merger agreement to enter into an agreement with a third party regarding a superior proposal, or the board changes its recommendation in response to a superior proposal and Parent terminates, the Company must pay Parent a $255 million termination fee.
event_claims.quote_text: If the Company terminates the merger agreement to enter into an agreement with a third party regarding a superior proposal or the board changes its recommendation in response to a superior proposal and the agreement is terminated by Parent, then the Company must pay a termination fee of $255 million to Parent

event_claims.description: The Company may terminate the merger agreement before the stockholder meeting to accept a superior proposal if it pays Parent a $255 million termination fee before or substantially concurrently with termination and promptly enters into a definitive agreement for the superior proposal.
event_claims.quote_text:
at any time prior to the Company stockholder meeting to accept a “superior proposal” (defined below under The Merger Agreement — Other Covenants and Agreements — No Solicitation ) so

long as (i) prior to or substantially concurrent with such termination, the Company has paid a termination fee of $255 million to Parent and (ii) promptly after such termination, the Company enters into a definitive agreement with respect to such superior proposal

event_claims.description: Parent may terminate the merger agreement if the board takes an action constituting a change of recommendation before the stockholder meeting to adopt the merger agreement.
event_claims.quote_text:
Parent may terminate the merger agreement:

•

if the board takes an action that constitutes a change of recommendation (as defined below under The Merger Agreement — Other Covenants and Agreements — No Solicitation ) prior to the stockholder meeting to adopt the merger agreement

event_claims.description: The Company will pay Parent a $255 million termination fee if the Company terminates the merger agreement before stockholder approval in response to a superior proposal and promptly enters into a definitive agreement for that proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: the Company has terminated the merger agreement before stockholder approval of the merger proposal is obtained in response to a superior proposal and promptly after such termination the Company enters into a definitive agreement with respect to such superior proposal

event_claims.description: The Company will pay Parent a $255 million termination fee if Parent terminates the merger agreement because of a change of recommendation.
event_claims.quote_text: Parent has terminated the merger agreement in the event of a change of recommendation

event_claims.description: The Company will pay Parent a $255 million termination fee if the merger is terminated under specified conditions and, concurrently or within 12 months after termination, the Company enters into or completes a qualifying transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: the merger is terminated under specified conditions and concurrently with or within 12 months after such termination, the Company enters into a definitive agreement providing for a qualifying transaction (as defined below under “ *The Merger Agreement — Termination Fees* ”) or completes a qualifying transaction
